page: Microsoft Azure E2_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server allocates 1 physical core with 2 threads per core via Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. The hardware configuration includes a 50 MB L3 cache, support for AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets, and 51 GB of local storage. No GPUs are included in this instance type.

Benchmark results indicate that the E2_v3 operates in the lower tier of performance metrics. Both single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, measuring 9.34 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 6.41 GB/sec for large-block write operations. These metrics highlight a significant performance tradeoff: while the instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the underlying compute and memory throughput are limited.

Given its hardware profile, the E2_v3 offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high CPU throughput. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers like Redis, lightweight web serving, and basic enterprise applications requiring more memory than compute power. It is not recommended for intensive compute tasks, large-scale LLM inference, or high-throughput data processing.
description: The Microsoft Azure E2_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization, it provides 1 physical core, 2 threads, and 51 GB of local storage, without GPU acceleration. While offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, benchmark data places its compute and memory bandwidth performance in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Specifically, stress-ng and Geekbench scores reflect limited processing power, and uncached memory read bandwidth is restricted to 9.34 GB/sec. Consequently, this instance is best suited for memory-optimized, low-compute workloads such as small databases, caching layers, and lightweight web serving where resource density is prioritized over raw processing speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, suitable for low-compute, memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2_v3 features 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 51 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight databases and caching workloads with moderate compute requirements.
